title: Capturing a Celestial Visitor
story: A bright streak blazed across the night sky, captivating stargazers. This wasn't just any comet—it was Donati's comet, named after the Italian astronomer who first spotted it. On September 28, 1858, history was made when this cosmic wanderer became the first comet ever captured in a photograph. This groundbreaking image opened a new chapter in astronomy, allowing scientists to study comets in ways never before possible.

activity_answer: It was the first photo of a comet

story: Bullets whizzed by as Theodore Roosevelt Jr. bravely led troops onto Utah Beach. The son of President Theodore Roosevelt showed incredible courage during the D-Day landings in 1944. title: Boxing Champ Holmes Triumphs in Vegas
story: Pow! Bam! The boxing ring in Las Vegas erupted with excitement on September 28, 1979. Larry Holmes, the reigning champ, faced off against tough challenger Earnie Shavers. The crowd held their breath as the fighters traded blows. In the 11th round, Holmes unleashed a flurry of punches. The referee stepped in, declaring Holmes the winner by TKO. title: Sweet Success: Jimmie Rodgers' Honeycomb Hit
story: Jimmie Rodgers' career buzzed with excitement in 1957. His catchy tune Honeycomb climbed to the top of the charts on September 28. The song's playful lyrics and upbeat melody captured listeners' hearts. Rodgers' smooth voice and guitar strumming created a musical treat as sweet as its namesake.
activity_question: What song by Jimmie Rodgers reached number one?
bonus_fact: Honeycomb is also a real food made by bees!
